codeReview.summary: The subscription module implementation covers most core features (plans, subscribe, cancel, coupons, mock payments) but has several critical blocking issues: (1) All code is untracked in git - the entire subscription module (backend and frontend) consists of untracked files that are not version controlled. (2) Server-side premium endpoint gating is NOT implemented - the require_premium_access dependency exists but is not used by any API endpoint, so premium content is not protected by 403 responses. (3) No tests were created despite TDD requirement in backend-worker skill. (4) Renewal reminder notification feature is missing.

issues.description: CRITICAL: All subscription module code is UNTRACKED in git. The entire implementation (router.py, models.py, schemas.py, service.py, repository.py for backend; pricing/page.tsx, PremiumGate.tsx, subscriptions.ts for frontend) consists of untracked files. This code is not version controlled and will not persist across git operations.

Server-side premium endpoint gating NOT implemented: The require_premium_access dependency exists in shared/dependencies.py but is not used by ANY API endpoint. The expected behavior states 'Feature gating: server-side 403 for premium endpoints' but no endpoint returns 403 when non-premium users access premium content. The dependency should be applied to endpoints like /study-plans/generate, /mock-tests/*, /questions/bank, etc.

No tests created for subscription module despite backend-worker skill requiring TDD. The skill explicitly states: 'Write tests first (TDD) - Create test file in apps/api/tests/', but no test_subscriptions.py exists. The handoff shows verification was done via curl only, not tests.

Expected behavior 'Renewal reminder: notification sent 3 days before expiry' is NOT implemented. There is no notification logic or background task that sends renewal reminders. The has_access_to_premium_content method returns upgrade_message without creating any notification records.

Coupon ID not persisted to subscription: When subscribe() applies a coupon, it calculates discount but sets coupon_id=None in the subscription record. The coupon_id should be saved to track which coupon was used for audit/payment purposes.

processMockPayment retrieves payment by subscription_id but the router endpoint path uses /payments/mock without including the payment_id. If a subscription has multiple payment attempts, the wrong payment could be retrieved. Consider passing payment_id explicitly or adding payment ID validation.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Backend-worker skill requires TDD approach with tests written before implementation. The subscription module has zero test files despite the skill's explicit TDD requirement. The skill states 'Write failing tests for the expected behavior (red phase)' but no tests were created.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: apps/api/tests/ contains test files for auth, admin_users, analytics, mock_test_engine, notifications, study_plans, syllabus but NO test_subscriptions.py. Handoff verification shows only curl commands were used, not pytest.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The feature is marked complete in features.json but no tests exist. The skill's TDD procedure was not followed. This appears to be a systemic issue - all recent features reviewed show no tests despite TDD requirement.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff shows 'tests: {added: [], coverage: Subscription endpoints verified via curl tests...' This is not TDD and not proper test coverage.

sharedStateObservations.observation: All new files in the subscription module are untracked in git. This breaks basic version control practices. If the branch is reset or code is pulled on another machine, the subscription module disappears.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: git status shows untracked: apps/api/internal/subscriptions/{router,models,schemas,service,repository}.py, apps/web/src/app/pricing/page.tsx, apps/web/src/components/ui/PremiumGate.tsx, apps/web/src/lib/api/subscriptions.ts

summary: FAIL - The subscription module has four blocking issues: (1) All code is untracked in git and will be lost, (2) server-side premium endpoint gating is not implemented despite being a required feature, (3) no tests were created despite TDD skill requirement, (4) renewal reminder notifications are missing. The core subscription flow (plans, subscribe, cancel, coupons, mock payments) is implemented correctly with appropriate pricing display (₹499/₹1199/₹3999 with correct 20%/33% savings labels), but critical infrastructure and integration pieces are absent.
